The wind howls with a fury that rivals my own, yet it carries no prophecy of death.
I glide above the rushing floodwaters, searching for the specific souls whose time has finally come.
The crashing earth drowns out my keen, making it impossible to warn the dying of their end.
So many lives flicker out at once that my voice cracks from the endless, overlapping wails.
I drift through the ruins of the village, marking each cold body with a final, piercing shriek.
The sky splits open with a light that tastes of copper and dying stars.
I feel the vibration of a great ending echoing through the marrow of my bones.
This celestial fire screams a melody of extinction that only my kind can truly hear.
I must gather the wind in my throat to match the wail of the falling heavens.
A cosmic shadow descends, and I prepare my voice to herald the coming silence of the world.
My wail shall echo through every stone of this land until the rightful bloodline is recognized.
You dare claim this soil when the scent of impending death already clings to your own threshold.
I will scream the secrets of your ancestors' graves if you do not move your markers back.
This boundary is marked by the tears of the fallen, not by the fences of the living.
If you persist in this theft, I shall announce your demise to the wind before the moon sets.
This shimmering rift tastes of a death that has not yet happened.
I shall let out a keen that will shatter the edges of this gateway.
My wail will echo through this void to warn the souls waiting beyond.
The air around this portal is silent, lacking the scent of impending doom.
I feel a cold wind from the other side calling for my lament.
These interlocking branches snag my shroud and dampen the resonance of my mourning wail.
I can smell the scent of a dying lineage drifting through this suffocating greenery.
My scream will tear through this heavy silence to announce the coming shadow.
The thick canopy swallows the moonlight, making it harder to track the doomed.
I glide above the brambles, searching for the specific heartbeat that signals a passing.
My throat grows dry and tight while I wait for the scent of impending death.
The air remains heavy and still, offering no soul to wail for in this void.
I ache to release a piercing shriek that would shatter the stillness of this eternity.
Long have I drifted through the mist without a single family line to announce.
This oppressive quiet is a torture far worse than any ghost's eternal wandering.
You dare threaten the one who heralds the inevitable end of your lineage.
My scream will tear the air from your lungs before your blade reaches me.
I have seen the silver cord of your life fraying in the spirit world.
Try to strike me and you shall only taste the cold wind of the grave.
Your threats are meaningless to a spirit who knows exactly when you will fall.
This silver comb is the only thing that keeps my wild hair smooth for the wailing.
I cherish this heavy shroud because it hides my form from those who cannot bear the sight.
No treasure compares to this ancient mirror that reflects the shimmering light of a dying soul.
I guard this ivory flute with my life, for its tone echoes the deepest grief of the bloodline.
This tattered cloak has traveled through a thousand frosts and witnessed a thousand final breaths.
My voice is waning and I must hoard my screams for the truly doomed.
There are too many dying breaths in this valley for my lungs to announce.
I cannot wail for every passing soul or I shall be silent when kings fall.
This ghostly energy is finite and I must choose which bloodlines deserve my cry.
If I spend my sorrow on the unimportant, I will have nothing left for the great.
